concepts.name: Algorithm
concepts.short_definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem or accomplishing a task.

concepts.name: Operation
concepts.short_definition: A single, fundamental action performed by an algorithm.

concepts.name: Input Size
concepts.short_definition: A measure of the size of the input to an algorithm, often denoted by 'n'.

concepts.name: Time Complexity
concepts.short_definition: A measure of how the execution time of an algorithm grows with the input size.

concepts.name: Big O Notation
concepts.short_definition: A mathematical notation used to describe the upper bound of an algorithm's time complexity.

concepts.name: Constant Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time does not depend on the input size.

concepts.name: Linear Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows linearly with the input size.

concepts.name: Quadratic Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows with the square of the input size.

concepts.name: Logarithmic Time
concepts.short_definition: An algorithm whose execution time grows logarithmically with the input size.

concepts.name: Worst Case
concepts.short_definition: The maximum amount of time an algorithm can take for a given input size.
